Crataeva fasciculata is a plant species native to certain regions, and it is also known as the 'varuna' plant. The health benefits of Crataeva fasciculata include its potential to help manage kidney stones, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Crataeva fasciculata include decoctions, infusions, and powders, often used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
